_meta.notes: Per-venue facts generated by scripts/generate-venue-facts.ts using Perplexity sonar-pro web search.

2028 Stadium.iconicMoments: Home stadium of the Los Angeles Rams and Los Angeles Chargers. Hosted Super Bowl LVI in 2022 and will host matches of the 2026 FIFA World Cup and Super Bowl LXI in 2027.
2028 Stadium.spectatorExperience: Modern indoor venue with translucent roof providing natural light. Features excellent sight lines across all seats in a compact bowl design.
2028 Stadium.changes2028: Configured with temporary stands for swimming events.

Alamitos Beach Stadium.iconicMoments: Purpose-built temporary stadium for the 2028 Games at the western edge of Long Beach's coastline. Hosts beach volleyball and blind football. Adjacent to downtown Long Beach.
Alamitos Beach Stadium.spectatorExperience: Open-air beachfront venue with wide-open spaces, soft sand beaches, and ocean views. Offers a relaxed coastal atmosphere for watching beach volleyball.
Alamitos Beach Stadium.changes2028: Temporary venue constructed specifically for the 2028 Games.

Belmont Shore.iconicMoments: Belmont Shore is a beachside area in Long Beach known for its waterfront along the Pacific Ocean and 11 miles of waterways connected to five miles of sandy beach. It has no prior Games history and is selected to host water sports for the 2028 Games.
Belmont Shore.spectatorExperience: Spectators watch open water swimming, coastal rowing, and sailing from along the sandy beach and waterfront with views over the Pacific Ocean. The setup features temporary infrastructure on the sand for close-up viewing of the competitions.
Belmont Shore.changes2028: Temporary infrastructure created along the sand for sailing board events and other water sports.

Carson Center Court.iconicMoments: The venue hosted the LA Women's Tennis Championships, a WTA Premier tournament, from 2003 to 2009. It is part of the Dignity Health Sports Park complex on the California State University, Dominguez Hills campus, which also serves as the home of the LA Galaxy soccer team.
Carson Center Court.spectatorExperience: Carson Center Court is an outdoor tennis arena with 360-degree seating around the court, offering spectators views from all angles. The venue is part of a larger multi-use sports complex that includes soccer facilities and a velodrome.
Carson Center Court.changes2028: The venue will host tennis competition during the 2028 Summer Games, with temporary court facilities (Carson Courts 1-11) constructed alongside the existing center court to accommodate the tournament schedule.

Carson Court 1.iconicMoments: Home to the USTA High-Performance Training Center since 2003. Hosted the LA Women's Tennis Championships, a WTA Premier tournament, from 2003 to 2009. Trains top American junior tennis players.
Carson Court 1.spectatorExperience: Outdoor tennis court with temporary seating. Part of the USTA training facilities offering close-up views of matches.
Carson Court 1.changes2028: Temporary seating added for the 2028 Games tennis competition.

Carson Court 2.iconicMoments: Home to the USTA High-Performance Training Center. Hosted the LA Women's Tennis Championships, a WTA Premier tournament, from 2003 to 2009. Will host tennis for the 2028 Summer Games and wheelchair tennis the following month.
Carson Court 2.spectatorExperience: Outdoor tennis stadium with 360-degree seating around the center court. Adjacent to the Dignity Health Sports Park soccer stadium.

Carson Courts 3-11.iconicMoments: Hosted the LA Women's Tennis Championships, a WTA Premier tournament, from 2003 to 2009. Home to the United States Tennis Association’s High-Performance Training Center. Record attendance of 11,168 set in 2016 for a Bernie Sanders campaign rally.
Carson Courts 3-11.spectatorExperience: Carson Center Court offers 360° seating around the outdoor tennis court. Courts 3-11 are existing facilities with temporary builds for additional match viewing.
Carson Courts 3-11.changes2028: Carson Courts 1-11 include temporary builds to expand capacity for the 2028 Games tennis events.

Carson Field.iconicMoments: Currently used as training grounds for the LA Galaxy soccer team. Will be transformed into a temporary venue for field hockey at the 2028 Games.
Carson Field.spectatorExperience: Temporary open-air venue configured for field hockey with modular seating around the pitch. Expect standard sight lines for the sport in a compact setup.
Carson Field.changes2028: Temporary venue built by transforming the existing LA Galaxy training grounds for the 2028 Games.

Carson Stadium.iconicMoments: Home to LA Galaxy since 2003, MLS Cup champions five times. Hosted Los Angeles Chargers temporarily after 2017 renovations. Second soccer-specific stadium built in MLS era.
Carson Stadium.spectatorExperience: Steep stands close to the pitch create an immersive atmosphere in this open-air stadium. Modular design allows capacity adjustments with temporary seating for larger events.

Carson Velodrome.iconicMoments: The site hosted track cycling at the 1984 Games on an outdoor concrete velodrome built in 1982 and demolished in 2003. The current indoor VELO Sports Center opened in 2004 as the only Olympic-standard velodrome in the United States. It serves as the national training center for USA Cycling and home track of world champion Sarah Hammer.
Carson Velodrome.spectatorExperience: Indoor venue maintained at 72°F and 50% humidity with 2,450 seats offering great sight lines to the 250-meter Siberian pine track banked up to 45 degrees. Spectators experience high-speed cycling up to 53 mph in a controlled environment.

Comcast Squash Center.iconicMoments: Squash makes its debut at the 2028 Games at this venue, which is built on the iconic Courthouse Square backlot at Universal Studios, famous worldwide for its role in the Back to the Future trilogy.
Comcast Squash Center.spectatorExperience: The venue is a temporary, purpose-built structure set against one of Hollywood's most recognizable film backdrops, offering spectators a unique blend of world-class sport and cinematic atmosphere.
Comcast Squash Center.changes2028: The Comcast Squash Center at Universal Studios is a temporary venue constructed specifically for the 2028 Games.

DTLA Arena.iconicMoments: Home to the Los Angeles Lakers, Los Angeles Sparks, and Los Angeles Kings. Hosted NBA Finals, Stanley Cup Finals, and Grammy Awards. Known as a hub for major concerts and entertainment events.
DTLA Arena.spectatorExperience: Indoor arena with excellent sight lines from all seats due to its circular design and central scoreboard. High-energy atmosphere with loud crowds for Lakers games and concerts.
DTLA Arena.changes2028: Rebranded as DTLA Arena for the 2028 Games.

Dodger Stadium.iconicMoments: Home of the Los Angeles Dodgers since 1962, the first privately financed MLB ballpark since Yankee Stadium in 1923. Hosted the Dodgers' first three years with the Los Angeles Angels as tenants before they moved to Anaheim in 1966. Site of 13 no-hitters, including two perfect games, and concerts by The Beatles, The Rolling Stones, Elton John, and Michael Jackson.
Dodger Stadium.spectatorExperience: Open-air ballpark with unobstructed views from all 56,000 seats across five levels. Multi-level parking and hillside location provide scenic backdrop with consistent sight lines.

Energizer Park.iconicMoments: Home of St. Louis City SC since their 2023 MLS debut. Hosted US women's national team first match there, a 1-0 friendly win over Ireland in 2022 before 22,000. US men's national team won 3-0 friendly against Uzbekistan in 2023 before 15,000.
Energizer Park.spectatorExperience: Open-air stadium with grass pitch 40 feet below street level, two tiers of seating, and clear plastic awning that protects from weather while trapping crowd noise. Every seat within 120 feet of the pitch for close views.
Energizer Park.changes2028: Renamed Energizer Park in 2025 under naming rights agreement with Energizer.

Etihad Park.location: Willets Point, Queens, New York City
Etihad Park.iconicMoments: Etihad Park is the first soccer-specific stadium in New York City and will serve as the permanent home of New York City FC starting with the 2027-28 season. The venue has been selected to host nine matches during the 2028 Summer Games soccer tournaments, including a men's quarter-final, marking a historic moment for the city.
Etihad Park.spectatorExperience: The stadium features a capacity of 25,000 with modern facilities designed for an intense atmosphere. Supporters will have access to over 3,400 safe standing positions in a dedicated section, a 9,000 square foot rooftop supporters porch bar, and a 40,000 square foot food hall featuring vendors from across New York City's boroughs.
Etihad Park.changes2028: During the 2028 Summer Games, the stadium will be temporarily renamed to New York Stadium in accordance with international sports governing body policy on corporate-sponsored names.

Exposition Park Stadium.iconicMoments: Completed in 2018 as Los Angeles' newest open-air stadium. Home to Angel City Football Club and Los Angeles Football Club, hosting national and global soccer matches.
Exposition Park Stadium.spectatorExperience: Open-air stadium adjacent to the LA Memorial Coliseum with views of the downtown Los Angeles skyline. Brings epic energy and hometown pride to events like flag football and lacrosse.

Fairgrounds Cricket Stadium.iconicMoments: The Fairplex has hosted the LA County Fair annually since 1922. Cricket returns to the 2028 Games for the first time in 128 years, making this venue the site of that historic return.
Fairgrounds Cricket Stadium.spectatorExperience: The cricket stadium is purpose-built within the sprawling 543-acre Fairplex grounds, which feature scenic parks, plazas, and picnic areas alongside the main venue.
Fairgrounds Cricket Stadium.changes2028: A new cricket stadium was constructed at the Fairplex specifically for the 2028 Games, with capacity for 6,000 spectators and potential expansion to 15,000 with temporary seating.

Galen Center.iconicMoments: Home to USC Trojans basketball and volleyball since its opening. The venue regularly hosts major events including the Grammy Awards and serves as a flexible multipurpose arena for concerts, pageants, and athletic competitions.
Galen Center.spectatorExperience: An indoor multipurpose arena with 10,258 seats and 22 private suites, offering climate-controlled viewing. The facility is designed for flexible configurations to accommodate different sports and entertainment events.
Galen Center.changes2028: The venue will be temporarily renamed USC Sports Center during the 2028 Games per naming-rights regulations, and will be reconfigured to host rhythmic gymnastics and badminton with temporary seating adjustments.

Geodis Park.iconicMoments: Geodis Park set the Major League Soccer single-game attendance record for a soccer-specific venue at its sold-out inaugural match on May 1, 2022. The stadium hosted the 2023 League's Cup final featuring Lionel Messi and was selected as one of twelve venues for the 2025 FIFA Club World Cup.
Geodis Park.spectatorExperience: The stadium features a 360-degree canopy that amplifies the game day experience, a 65-foot-wide shared concourse, and natural grass playing surface. Spectators benefit from exposed steel and mass timber design elements throughout the venue, with multiple entry points including a dedicated supporters group gate on the north side.
Geodis Park.changes2028: No changes reported for the 2028 Games; Geodis Park continues standard operation as Nashville SC's home venue.

Honda Center.iconicMoments: Home to the Anaheim Ducks of the National Hockey League since 1993. Hosted first event with Barry Manilow in 1993. Will host volleyball for the 2028 Summer Games.
Honda Center.spectatorExperience: Indoor arena with 84 luxury suites and marble-lined concourses. Good sight lines for basketball at 18,609 seats and hockey at 17,732 seats.
Honda Center.changes2028: Surrounded by ocV!BE neighborhood development completing by 2028 with new parking, plazas, and entertainment facilities.

Industry Hills MTB Course.iconicMoments: Purpose-built for the 2028 Games mountain bike competition adjacent to Pacific Palms resort. Relocated from initial plans at Frank G. Bonelli Regional Park. Course designed as 4-6km twisting layout with drops, jumps, and varied terrain.
Industry Hills MTB Course.spectatorExperience: Open-air hilltop venue in rugged San Gabriel Valley terrain with spectator areas close to the action on climbs, descents, drops, and jumps. Features rolling elevations from 550 to 750 feet for dynamic viewing.
Industry Hills MTB Course.changes2028: Temporary structure constructed within hillside of existing golf course as part of site improvement plan, to remain as legacy.

Intuit Dome.iconicMoments: Home of the LA Clippers since the 2024-25 NBA season. Scheduled to host the 2026 NBA All-Star Game. Selected for basketball at the 2028 Summer Games.
Intuit Dome.spectatorExperience: Indoor arena with the Halo Board, the world's largest indoor 4K scoreboard spanning nearly an acre. Features The Wall of 51 uninterrupted rows and 4500 baseline seats creating intense crowd energy.
Intuit Dome.changes2028: Retains Intuit Dome naming rights for the basketball events.

LA Convention Center Hall 1.iconicMoments: Served as Main Press Headquarters during the 1984 Games. Hosts major conventions, trade shows, and exhibitions annually.
LA Convention Center Hall 1.spectatorExperience: Indoor exhibit hall with 36-foot ceilings and flexible seating configurations for theater-style events up to several thousand. Multi-purpose space designed for clear sight lines in sports setups like fencing and taekwondo.
LA Convention Center Hall 1.changes2028: Configured with limited seating for fencing and taekwondo events.

LA Convention Center Hall 2.iconicMoments: Served as Main Press Headquarters during the 1984 Games. Hosts hundreds of annual conventions, trade shows, and exhibitions. Filming location for TV shows and movies.
LA Convention Center Hall 2.spectatorExperience: Indoor exhibit hall with high ceilings and flexible configurations for spectator seating. Multi-purpose space adapted for close-up action in combat sports like judo and wrestling.
LA Convention Center Hall 2.changes2028: By early 2028, expansion project will complete 80 percent of work including finished exterior facade, with South and West Halls restored to pre-construction footprints for Games use.

LA Convention Center Hall 3.iconicMoments: Part of the Los Angeles Convention Center, which served as the Main Press Headquarters during the 1984 Games. Hosts hundreds of major conventions, trade shows, and exhibitions annually.
LA Convention Center Hall 3.spectatorExperience: Indoor exhibit hall with column-free space and polished concrete floor, configured for table tennis with theater-style seating. Multi-functional space used for large events with pre-function and registration areas.
LA Convention Center Hall 3.changes2028: Configured as a competition venue for table tennis.

LA Memorial Coliseum.iconicMoments: Hosted the 1932 Summer Games opening and closing ceremonies, track and field, gymnastics, field hockey, and equestrian show jumping. Site of the 1984 Summer Games athletics where Carl Lewis won four gold medals. Hosted Super Bowls I and VII, the 1959 World Series with record attendance, and USC Trojans football since 1923.
LA Memorial Coliseum.spectatorExperience: Open-air elliptical concrete bowl with terraced seating and a grand peristyle arch at the east end featuring the Court of Honor. Renovated in 2019 with new seats, increased legroom, wider aisles, and restored peristyle for improved sight lines.

Long Beach Aquatics Center.iconicMoments: Original Belmont Plaza Pool opened in 1968 and hosted two Olympic Trials meets. Site of the pool that served Long Beach aquatics for decades until seismic issues led to its closure and demolition in 2014. Replacement Belmont Beach and Aquatics Center under construction for completion by spring 2028.
Long Beach Aquatics Center.spectatorExperience: Shaded seating area with 544 seats surrounds the 50-meter all-deep outdoor pool. Open-air venue features a movable bulkhead for competitions, four springboards, a separate instructional pool, and a splash pad.
Long Beach Aquatics Center.changes2028: Temporary open-air stadium configuration as Long Beach Aquatics Center built in the Long Beach Convention Center Lot for the 2028 Games.

Long Beach Arena.iconicMoments: Hosted 1984 Games volleyball championships. Home to Long Beach Ice Dogs hockey team from 1990 to 2007 and early Los Angeles Kings games in 1967. Site of live album recordings by Led Zeppelin, Iron Maiden, and others.
Long Beach Arena.spectatorExperience: Indoor arena with 77-foot-high ceiling, large scoreboard, and LED screen. Cozier atmosphere than larger venues, fostering strong energy between performers and crowd.

Long Beach Climbing Theater.iconicMoments: Purpose-built temporary venue for the 2028 Games climbing competition in the Long Beach Convention Center Lot. Features the Pacific Ocean as a backdrop. Part of the multi-sport temporary setups in the area including aquatics.
Long Beach Climbing Theater.spectatorExperience: Open-air theater with climbing walls framed by ocean views. Spectators experience coastal winds and marine scenery during events.
Long Beach Climbing Theater.changes2028: Temporary structure constructed specifically for the 2028 Games.

Long Beach Target Shooting Hall.iconicMoments: Part of the Long Beach Convention Center, which hosted Fencing during the 1984 Games. The arena has hosted concerts, sports events, and conventions over decades.
Long Beach Target Shooting Hall.spectatorExperience: Indoor arena with configurable seating up to 13,500, offering tiered sight lines focused on the floor where the temporary shooting range will be installed. Located on the downtown waterfront with coastal views accessible nearby.
Long Beach Target Shooting Hall.changes2028: Temporary purpose-built indoor range installed within the Long Beach Arena at the Convention Center for rifle and pistol events.

Marine Stadium.iconicMoments: Hosted the rowing events for the 1932 Summer Games as the first manmade rowing course in the United States. Also hosted the United States rowing trials for the 1968 Summer Games.
Marine Stadium.spectatorExperience: Open-air marine venue with viewing from shores and bleachers along the 1500-meter course. Spectators experience close-up action on the calm, protected waters with a relaxed coastal atmosphere.
Marine Stadium.changes2028: Will use a 1500m course instead of the standard 2000m due to a bridge built in 1955.

OKC Softball Park.iconicMoments: Hosts the Women's College World Series annually since 1990, with events booked through 2035. Home to the Oklahoma City Spark professional softball team from 2023 to 2024. Site of Olympic trials for the USA Softball Women’s National Team.
OKC Softball Park.spectatorExperience: Open-air softball stadium with 13,000 seats including an upper deck, offering a carnival-like atmosphere with food trucks and proximity to the National Softball Hall of Fame and Museum. Features four fields for simultaneous games, jumbo video screen, and upgraded amenities like suites and restrooms.
OKC Softball Park.changes2028: No changes for the 2028 Games reported.

OKC Whitewater Center.iconicMoments: Opened in 2016 as the world's first pumped whitewater park with recreational, competition, and freestyle channels. Part of the Boathouse District, headquarters for USA Canoe and Kayak Federation and a U.S. training center. Hosted professional training and public rafting since opening.
OKC Whitewater Center.spectatorExperience: Open-air venue with covered patios and decks overlooking the rapids, kayak slalom, and Oklahoma River. Elevated viewing from two-story center with sightlines to whitewater action and downtown OKC skyline.

PayPal Park.iconicMoments: Home of the San Jose Earthquakes of Major League Soccer since its opening in 2015. Hosted the team's first match, a 2-1 win over Chicago Fire on March 22, 2015. Also home to Bay FC of the National Women’s Soccer League.
PayPal Park.spectatorExperience: Open-air soccer-specific stadium with steepest-raked seating in MLS for close-up views from all seats. Features a canopy roof, field-level suites and club seats, largest outdoor bar in North America behind one goal, and a 2-acre fan zone.

Peacock Theater.iconicMoments: Formerly Nokia Theatre L.A. Live and Microsoft Theater. Hosts American Music Awards, ESPYs, Primetime Emmy Awards, BET Awards, MTV Video Music Awards, MTV Movie Awards, and Rock N’ Roll Hall of Fame Induction Ceremonies.
Peacock Theater.spectatorExperience: Indoor theater with 7,100 seats and no seat further than 220 feet from the stage, providing mid-sized intimacy. Features four levels of lobbies for concessions and VIP areas.

Port of Los Angeles.iconicMoments: Hosted sailing events during the 1932 Summer Games in the Outer Harbor. Will host dinghy, multihull, and skiff sailing for the 2028 Games.
Port of Los Angeles.spectatorExperience: Open-air waterfront venue with temporary stands along the Pacific Ocean harbor. Offers views of boats racing in the harbor amid industrial port surroundings.
Port of Los Angeles.changes2028: Existing venue equipped with temporary stands for the 2028 Games.

Riviera Country Club.iconicMoments: Hosted the U.S. Open in 1948 won by Ben Hogan, PGA Championships in 1983 won by Hal Sutton and 1995 won by Steve Elkington, U.S. Senior Open in 1998 won by Hale Irwin, and U.S. Amateur in 2017 won by Doc Redman. Primary host of the Genesis Invitational since 1926. Will host U.S. Women's Open in 2026.
Riviera Country Club.spectatorExperience: Open-air golf course in a valley near the Pacific Ocean with elevation changes from 180 to 330 feet. Walkable layout designed by George C. Thomas Jr. and William P. Bell, known for strategic Golden Age architecture.

Rose Bowl Aquatics Center.iconicMoments: Opened in 1990 on the site of the former Brookside Plunge. Training facility for Rose Bowl Aquatics swim club, masters swimming, diving teams, and water polo club. Selected to host diving for the 2028 Summer Games.
Rose Bowl Aquatics Center.spectatorExperience: Open-air facility with two Olympic-sized pools and a diving well adjacent to Rose Bowl Stadium. Spectators experience poolside viewing in a community park setting with dry land facilities nearby.
Rose Bowl Aquatics Center.changes2028: Requires minimal modifications for diving events while adhering to international standards.

Rose Bowl Stadium.iconicMoments: Hosts the annual Rose Bowl college football game since 1923, known as the granddaddy of them all. Site of five Super Bowls from 1977 to 1993 and hosted soccer matches at the 1984 Games. Home field for UCLA Bruins football since 1982.
Rose Bowl Stadium.spectatorExperience: Open-air stadium with a complete bowl shape featuring a uniform rim and concrete colonnade. Provides sight lines across more than 92,000 seats in an elliptical design originally influenced by the Yale Bowl.

Santa Anita Park.iconicMoments: Hosted horse riding events for the 1984 Summer Games. Served as a temporary assembly center for Japanese Americans during World War II. Hosted the Breeders' Cup eleven times, more than any other racetrack.
Santa Anita Park.spectatorExperience: Open-air venue with a 1,100-foot Art Deco grandstand seating 26,000 and an infield park area with picnic tables and trees holding 50,000 more, against the backdrop of the San Gabriel Mountains.
Santa Anita Park.changes2028: Chosen in April 2025 to host equestrian events for the 2028 Summer Games.

Scotts Miracle-Gro Field.iconicMoments: Opened in 2021 as Lower.com Field, replacing Historic Crew Stadium, which was the first soccer-specific stadium in Major League Soccer when it opened in 1999. The venue has hosted the 2024 MLS All-Star Game, multiple U.S. Men's National Team matches, and the 2024 SheBelieves Cup.
Scotts Miracle-Gro Field.spectatorExperience: A full canopy covers all 20,371 seats while leaving the pitch open to the sky, providing weather protection while maintaining an open-air atmosphere. The stadium features a heated hybrid grass pitch and includes 30 suites and 1,900 club seats, with a 40,000-square-foot landscaped outdoor plaza adjacent to the venue.
Scotts Miracle-Gro Field.changes2028: No venue-specific changes for 2028 reported; the stadium operates under its current configuration as home to the Columbus Crew.

Snapdragon Stadium.iconicMoments: Built on the site of the former Qualcomm Stadium, which hosted NFL Chargers games and MLB Padres for decades. Home to San Diego State Aztecs football since 2022, with its debut game drawing nearly full capacity against Arizona Wildcats. Hosts San Diego Wave FC of NWSL and new MLS team San Diego FC.
Snapdragon Stadium.spectatorExperience: Open-air stadium with landscaped outdoor concourses, a 5,000-seat student section including 2,900 safe-standing seats for intense atmosphere, and premium options like loge boxes and social decks with field views. Lower deck encloses the field with upper decks on both sides, plus extensive catering and cashless concessions.

Trestles State Beach.location: San Onofre State Beach, between San Diego and Orange counties
Trestles State Beach.iconicMoments: Surfers began discovering Trestles in 1933 for its year-round waves considered among the best in America. The area became part of San Onofre State Beach in 1971 and is recognized as central to the establishment of surfing as a Southern California cultural identity and lifestyle.
Trestles State Beach.spectatorExperience: Spectators watch from natural beach settings with views of consistent, near-perfect waves across multiple breaks (Upper, Middle, and Lower Trestles). The venue offers an open-air coastal experience with the aesthetics of clean water and a largely natural environment.
Trestles State Beach.changes2028: The venue will host surfing competition for the 2028 Summer Games, marking its first use as an official Games venue.

Valley Complex 1.location: Sepulveda Basin Recreation Area, San Fernando Valley
Valley Complex 1.iconicMoments: Temporary venue purpose-built for the 2028 Games. Located in Los Angeles’ second largest urban park, which includes indoor and outdoor sports facilities, golf courses, and wildlife areas. First time events will be held in the San Fernando Valley for the 2028 Summer Games.
Valley Complex 1.spectatorExperience: Open-air venue designed for BMX Freestyle and Skateboarding Street events. Spectators experience adrenaline-spiking and gravity-defying runs in a park setting.
Valley Complex 1.changes2028: Temporary open-air venue built specifically for the 2028 Games.

Valley Complex 2.location: Sepulveda Basin Recreation Area, San Fernando Valley
Valley Complex 2.iconicMoments: Temporary venue purpose-built for the 2028 Games. Located in Los Angeles’ second largest urban park, which features indoor and outdoor sports facilities, golf courses, and wildlife areas.
Valley Complex 2.spectatorExperience: Open-air venue in an intimate setting where spectators are close to the action-packed Modern Pentathlon and Skateboarding Park events.
Valley Complex 2.changes2028: Temporary structure built specifically for Modern Pentathlon and Skateboarding Park.

Valley Complex 3.location: Sepulveda Basin Recreation Area, San Fernando Valley
Valley Complex 3.iconicMoments: Temporary venue purpose-built for the 2028 Games in Los Angeles’ second largest urban park.
Valley Complex 3.spectatorExperience: Open-air octagonal structure allows spectators to view 3x3 Basketball from all angles. Located amid sports facilities, golf courses, picnic areas, and wildlife spaces.
Valley Complex 3.changes2028: Temporary octagonal venue constructed for 3x3 Basketball in the Sepulveda Basin Recreation Area.

Valley Complex 4.location: Sepulveda Basin Recreation Area, San Fernando Valley
Valley Complex 4.iconicMoments: Built as part of a U.S. Army Corps of Engineers project in response to the 1938 floods. Hosts BMX racing, archery, and skateboarding for the 2028 Games as a temporary venue in Los Angeles’ second largest urban park.
Valley Complex 4.spectatorExperience: Built into the natural terrain for up-close views of the action. Outdoor setting in a large recreation area with sports facilities, golf courses, and parkland.
Valley Complex 4.changes2028: Temporary venue constructed in open park area adjacent to Balboa Lake for BMX racing and other events.

Venice Beach.iconicMoments: Venice Beach is known for its iconic boardwalk, canals, and vibrant street culture in Los Angeles. It serves as the triathlon venue for the 2028 Games with the transition area and finish line at Venice Boulevard meeting the sand.
Venice Beach.spectatorExperience: Open-air beachfront setting with ocean views, sand, and surrounding streets creating a casual, energetic atmosphere for watching athletes transition from swim to bike to run. Crowds gather along the shore and boulevard amid the neighborhood's lively boardwalk vibe.
Venice Beach.changes2028: Triathlon events relocated from Long Beach to Venice Beach.

Venice Beach Boardwalk.iconicMoments: Founded by Abbot Kinney in 1905 as Venice of America, a beach resort with canals, a pier, and Venetian architecture. Known for Muscle Beach, skatepark, art walls, and volleyball courts, drawing 10 million visitors yearly. Second-largest tourist attraction in Southern California after Disneyland.
Venice Beach Boardwalk.spectatorExperience: Open-air two-mile promenade parallel to the beach, connecting to Santa Monica in the north and Marina del Rey in the south. Lively pedestrian path with beach views, bike path, street performers, and oceanfront energy.

Whittier Narrows Clay Center.location: South El Monte, southeast Los Angeles County
Whittier Narrows Clay Center.iconicMoments: Whittier Narrows Clay Shooting Center is one of Southern California's premier shotgun ranges, long established as a favorite among competitive shooters. The facility will host trap and skeet events during the 2028 Games, marking a return of shooting sports to the Los Angeles area after their absence from recent Games cycles.
Whittier Narrows Clay Center.spectatorExperience: The sprawling open-air campus is set against the rolling greenery of the Whittier Narrows Recreation Area, providing a dramatic outdoor backdrop for shotgun competitions. Spectators will experience the natural landscape and open sightlines typical of a clay shooting range environment.
Whittier Narrows Clay Center.changes2028: The venue will be configured to host Olympic trap and skeet events with temporary spectator facilities added to accommodate Games-level attendance.
